title: The Topic Marker
explanation: は (wa) identifies the topic or subject of the sentence.
example_jp: わたしは ミラーです
pakka_tip: Written as 'ha' but pronounced 'wa' when used as a particle!

title: Negatives
logic_formula: [N1] は [N2] じゃありません
explanation: Use じゃありません to say "is not" or "am not".
example_jp: わたしは せんせいじゃありません
pakka_tip: In formal speech, use 'では ありません' (dewa arimasen).

title: Questions... ですか
explanation: Adding か at the end of a sentence creates a question.
example_jp: あなたは がくせい ですか
pakka_tip: Japanese sentences don't need a '?' if they end in 'ka'.

title: Also / Too
explanation: も (mo) replaces は when saying "also" or "too".
example_jp: ミラーさんも かいしゃいんです
example_en: Mr. Miller is also a company employee.
pakka_tip: You cannot use 'wa' and 'mo' together on the same word.
